title: SaaS Support Platform: 89% Inference Cost Reduction
subtitle: Fine-tuned Llama 3.1 8B replacing GPT-4o for customer support automation
description: A B2B SaaS company was spending $38K/month on OpenAI API calls to power their customer support automation — answering tickets, classifying intent, and drafting replies. We fine-tuned a Llama 3.1 8B model on three years of their support history, deployed it on vLLM with two A10G instances, and reduced their monthly AI spend to $4,200. P95 latency dropped from 2.1s to 680ms.

portfolio.projectSummary:
The client's support automation was built on GPT-4o — fast to ship, expensive to run. Roughly 1.2M API calls per month at an average of 800 tokens each had pushed their monthly bill to $38K, and with ticket volume climbing there was no ceiling in sight. Every ticket touched the model multiple times: classifying intent, drafting a reply, and routing it to the right queue.

We started by auditing the workload to confirm a smaller model could carry it, then fine-tuned a Llama 3.1 8B model on three years of the client's resolved support history, using LoRA adapters via Unsloth for fast, memory-efficient training iterations. Before anything reached production, the fine-tuned model had to clear a quality gate: a benchmark of 2,000 stratified held-out tickets covering the long tail of their distribution — unusual ticket types, angry customers, multi-language queries. It matched or exceeded GPT-4o on 96% of those cases.

We deployed the model on two AWS A10G instances via vLLM, tuning continuous batching and INT8 quantization to hold 680ms P95 latency — a 3x improvement over the prior 2.1s — across 1.2M monthly calls. Self-hosted inference runs at roughly $0.08 per million tokens versus $15 on GPT-4o, cutting monthly AI spend from $38K to $4,200 (about $405,000 saved per year). The full migration took seven weeks from audit to production, and the client now runs their support AI on infrastructure they own and control.

challenges.title: Quality Bar Without Regression
challenges.description: The client needed confidence that the fine-tuned model wouldn't introduce quality regressions on edge cases — unusual ticket types, angry customers, multi-language queries. We built a benchmark of 2,000 stratified test cases covering the long tail of their ticket distribution and required 95%+ parity before signing off on production deployment.

challenges.title: Latency Requirements
challenges.description: The existing GPT-4o setup averaged 2.1s P95 latency, which was already a UX concern. The self-hosted vLLM deployment needed to match or beat this. Through continuous batching configuration and quantization to INT8, we achieved 680ms P95 — a 3x improvement alongside the 89% cost reduction.

challenges.title: Migrating Without Downtime
challenges.description: Support automation runs in production around the clock — a risky place to swap out the model powering it. We ran the fine-tuned Llama model in shadow mode against live GPT-4o traffic, comparing outputs on real tickets without affecting customers, until the parity numbers held steady. Only then did we cut over, keeping GPT-4o available as an instant fallback throughout the transition.

approach.title: Inference Workload Audit
approach.description: We mapped every model call in the support pipeline — intent classification, reply drafting, ticket routing — along with its token footprint, establishing the $38K/month baseline and the exact quality bar a replacement would have to clear.

approach.title: Dataset Construction from Support History
approach.description: Three years of resolved tickets were curated into instruction-tuning pairs, stratified across intent types, languages, and resolution patterns so the fine-tuned model would learn the long tail, not just the common cases.

approach.title: LoRA Fine-Tuning with Unsloth
approach.description: We trained Llama 3.1 8B with LoRA adapters via Unsloth, which made training runs fast and memory-efficient enough to iterate through several configurations and evaluate each against the held-out benchmark.

approach.title: Benchmark Gate Against GPT-4o
approach.description: We required 95%+ parity with GPT-4o on 2,000 stratified held-out tickets before signing off on production. The final model reached 96%, with particular attention paid to the edge cases where regressions would be most visible.

approach.title: Self-Hosted Serving on vLLM
approach.description: The model went live on two AWS A10G instances under vLLM, with continuous batching and INT8 quantization tuned to meet both the throughput target (1.2M calls/month) and the sub-second latency target.

technicalSolution.title: Architecture Overview
technicalSolution.description: The fine-tuned Llama 3.1 8B model serves behind the client's existing support pipeline via vLLM on two AWS A10G instances. LangSmith provides tracing and evaluation across every inference call, giving the team visibility the prior API setup never exposed.

technicalSolution.title: Throughput & Latency Tuning
technicalSolution.description: Continuous batching lets the two-instance deployment absorb 1.2M calls per month, while INT8 quantization shrinks the model's memory and compute footprint. Together they hold P95 latency at 680ms — three times faster than the 2.1s GPT-4o baseline.

technicalSolution.title: Quality Guardrails
technicalSolution.description: The 2,000-ticket held-out benchmark doubles as a regression suite, and LangSmith eval traces flag drift on edge cases — multi-language queries, escalations, unusual ticket types — so quality is monitored continuously rather than assumed.

useCases.title: Ticket Intent Classification
useCases.description: Every incoming ticket is categorized by intent in milliseconds, with accuracy on par with the prior GPT-4o setup.

useCases.title: Automated Reply Drafting
useCases.description: The model drafts context-aware replies grounded in three years of the client's own support voice, ready for an agent to review or send.

useCases.title: Ticket Routing
useCases.description: Tickets are routed to the right queue automatically, reducing manual triage and improving time-to-first-response.

useCases.title: Edge-Case Quality Assurance
useCases.description: A held-out benchmark and live eval traces continuously catch regressions on the hard cases — angry customers, multi-language tickets, and rare intents.

useCases.title: Right-Sized the Model
useCases.description: We profiled 1.2M monthly GPT-4o calls and proved a fine-tuned Llama 3.1 8B could match frontier quality on their support tasks at a fraction of the token cost.

useCases.title: Self-Hosted on vLLM
useCases.description: Deployed on two AWS A10G instances with continuous batching, dropping P95 latency to 680ms while eliminating per-token API fees entirely.
